domain: Enterprise SaaS / B2B Document Processing Hub

rules.headings: tracking-tight, font-semibold, never use Inter for headings.

layout_and_spacing.strategy: Dense, clean, control-room style with massive reduction in visible elements.
rules: No cards for summary stats unless absolutely critical (e.g., just 'Pending Review' count).
Grid gaps should be tight (gap-4) to keep information structured.
Containers should use 1px subtle borders instead of drop shadows.

tabs.directive: MANDATORY: Implement a top-level Tab architecture separating 'Accounting' and 'Sales'.
tabs.style: Use Shadcn Tabs. Background should be transparent, active state gets a white bottom border or subtle white background.

tables.directive: Radically simplify columns. Remove Ref Intel, Freight GL, Validation, Routing, Source.
tables.style: Flat, no vertical dividers, subtle horizontal borders. Left-align all text.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. NO BACKEND CHANGES: Do not touch APIs, AI, LLM logic, workflows, or connections.
2. UI SIMPLIFICATION: The user feels the app is too cluttered. Remove the developer metrics (Validation Rate, Vendor Auto-Resolve, etc.) from the dashboard.

3. TABBED ROUTING: In the main document processing view, create two distinct tabs using Shadcn Tabs: 'Accounting' (for AP Workflow, Invoices, vendor resolution) and 'Sales' (for PO review, dunnage, bounds checking).
4. TABLE CLEANUP: In the Documents list, remove the 10-column layout. Keep only essential columns: Document, Type, Status, Date, Actions.
5. FILTER CLEANUP: Remove excessive checkbox filters, category buttons, and double date pickers. A simple search bar and a single status dropdown are enough.
6. SIDEBAR REDUCTION: Strip the 8 sidebar items down to the bare minimum (e.g., Documents, Settings).
7. SALES & INVENTORY CLEANUP: Remove the 6 confusing tabs (Rep Performance, Inventory Ledger, etc.). Bring the necessary views into the new top-level 'Sales' tab.
8. TEST IDs: Ensure all interactive elements have data-testid attributes (e.g., data-testid='tab-accounting', data-testid='tab-sales').
